content.answer:

following key topics

Slide 4: Think VN will spend 12m more. Do you see an updated topline from
VN FP&A?>> Additional spending: Per latest update from local that platform
ingested additional $13.5mil for Platform wise FSV and Voucher Rebate during
Feb 1-10 & Feb 25-28 (mainly for Feb 25th peak).Current EOM proj.: only
factored in Feb 1-10, Feb 25-28th yet, if to include, expect to be 109%
runrate (change from current EOM projection at 108% runrate), MP PC2 worse
off another 100K


Slide 5: Then will CN SIP be excluded from MP? >> Yes, CN SIP has been
split out from MP in this viewSlide 13: Can show me: 1) overall actual vs
target; 2) incubation vs mature P&L; 3) by market topline/ABS?

Hi Ian and Jianghong,

Pls find the point 3 follow up in attached deck, key conclusion
1) Despite UE improved with maturity, mature seller UE still not breakeven,
which is the key problem of worse off UE
2) Mar projects to be 3.7K ADO with -80K seller P&L (UE at -0.7 usd/order),
this aggressive improved from Jan -1.9usd/order mainly driven by mature
sellers rebaseline to breakeven

plus key next step
1. Evaluate Swarm Seller/SKU fundamentals (SKU uniqueness, pricing, sellers
ops capabilities), followed by SKU base and market push effectiveness,
*including
TW* *(ETA: Feb 20th)*
2. Monitor order change against P&L optimization

Best,

*Faye Yan*

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Pls find our replies as follows.



Slide 2

Do we know why we need to lose so much and still cannot sell? Views or CR
issue? Or simply SKUs not suitable?

vs SIP, can see the below table.

 for CR, it’s mainly contributed by high PO% and low sold count, besides
we're further analyzing 1)SKU uniqueness and 2)price competitiveness

 Table 1: Jan’26 Swarm vs SIP SKU/view/ADO performance

[image: image.png]

Slide 3

We expect incubation UE to get even worse?

building (6 role models push to 100 ADO each);  the previous incubation
batch to follow our strategy of -2usd and -1usd respectively for ages 2
months and 3 months..



What are next steps exactly?

months: -3/-2/-1 USD/pc; mature sellers breakeven).
Operations: Expand SKU pool with more batches and optimize seller PO%

P&L: Raise mature SKUs’ UE to breakeven and profit by Jun ’26 to offset
incubation costs.



How do the numbers here compare with our direct acquisition of TB sellers?
For both new and existing sellers?

Shopee vs direct acquisition, but the pool is smaller than existing mature
sellers

Definition: “new” refers to  shop onboarded to Shopee less than 3 months,
benchmarking Swarm apple to apple

[image: image.png]Best,

*Faye Yan*

*Address:* 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265

Slide 3: What is the definition of "New" and "Existing", especially for
Swarm in this table?

Slide 3 - 4: Swarm take rate is lower than lever 1 & 2. If I remove lever
1, which consists mostly of beauty and fashion sellers with a high take
rate, the comparison against level 2 is still very low. So the spending of
Swarm are mostly rebate instead of ads, but that cannot gel with slide 4.

Do you have the market level view for slide 4?

unique?

For the unique SKUs with good CR, what are the commonalities we can draw?

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 4

   - Are we over-investing in Ads?

Feels like the ROAS does not make sense
   right now and affects our P&L tremendously



Slide 6

   - For exact match, Non-OF is better than CNCB in several markets?

I
   cannot reconcile the top and bottom tables in terms of NPC
   - For OF, what is the P&L right now?

Slide 7

   - I am really not sure the value OF brings to us
      - They have more unique SKUs
      - But their CR % is lower
      - Their NPC is bad
      - Their PO is bad


Others

   - It is not clear to me
      - For Match SKUs, how our NPC/CR/PO%/P&L compare with CNCB
      - For Unique SKUs, how our CR/P&L looks and trends like and do we see
      a way out here
      - OF value to us
   - I think we need to relook
      - SKUs that we choose to incubate and push
      - Whether we are spending way too much on ads
      - In general, think we can be more selective on the above instead of
      pushing everything

content.answer:
Hi Ian and Jianghong,

To summarize our conclusion from the meeting regarding the push direction
for Swarm. Let us know if further to clarify.

*Swarm serves the purpose of bringing in new assortments to our platform,
hence,*

   -

   For Unique SKUs/Sellers, shall be our main focus to push with following
   two guidelines
   -

      Build up role models with incubation and mature push plans
      -

      Clear path on pushing effectiveness (e.g. CR uplift) and long term
      profitability possibility
      -

   For SKUs that are inevitably brought to the platform that are existing
   Shopee’s SKUs, we shall target with at least P&L breakeven from the very
   beginning and earn money to sponsor the unique sellers/SKUs in 3 months
   -

   Specifically for OF, we will target to breakeven from Mar'26 and run
   organically


*With this in mind,*

   -

   From SIP P&L team, pricing accurately from the time when SKUs in, based
   on unique SKUs pool and mature stage to allocate marketing budget
   -

   From SIP Mkt team, 1) selectively choose role model and define push plan

identify BD focus with uniqueness and demand
   -

   From CN BD team, acquire targeted sellers by 1) built up role model 2)
   BD focus of SIP mkt team provided pattern


*Next Step:*

   -

   SIP Swarm team to adjust operations plan based on above alignment
   -

   SIP Swarm team to re-budget for upcoming month performance and update
   progress in weekly report


Best,

*Faye Yan*

Shopee | Cross Border

*Mobile:* +65 87767030

*Address:* 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265
